content:
Args from unknown skill: redesign the the ui, and do a code review of it as well.  organize it into two broad categories:  Life (containing goals, todos and the summary board) and System (containing the observability pages), ensure that any page visited is represented in the URL so reloading will bring me right back to that page.  Use a minimalist modern design with good typography, include both a light dark mode with a toggle.  this ui will house other things soon (budgeting, etc.) so there will be other top-level pages, APIs, etc.; ensure both the visual design and architecture allow for easy additions

Look at some images in /refs for inspiration, but don't be constrained by their color themes.

Allow for finer granularity of data / windows, it should be down to per-hour at least wherever possible.  Support drill-in for things like tools, hooks, etc.  It should be possible to see the last N invocations of a particular tool when clicking on a row, or a breakdown by arguments, something similar for hooks.  

are hooks / tools just snapshots?  I'd like to retain data from previous sessions and be able to browse later.

for memory, I'd like to also be able to see more detail about what's stored:  i should be able to see the actual memories by type or tag when clicking on it, be able to search by keyword, and eventually see that visualized (a graph, or however makes sense).  I need to see your own usage of it, e.g. how many things were stored, how many retrieved, etc.
